title: $formmodality
text: $formmodality Return an indication of the form modality for the requested form instance. $formmodality { ( InstanceName ) } Parameters Table 1. Parameters Parameter Data Type Description InstanceName String Component instance name; optional. If omitted, the modality of the current form instance is returned. Return Values Table 2. Values returned by $formmodality Value Meaning 1 InstName is a modal form. 0 InstName is a non-modal form. "" An error occurred. If an error occurs, $procerror contains a negative value that identifies the exact error. Table 3. Values of $procerror Commonly Returned Following $formmodality Value Error constant Meaning -57 <UACTERR_NO_INSTANCE> The named instance cannot be found in the component pool. -1105 <UPROCERR_INSTANCE> The instance name provided is not valid (For more information, see the newinstance ProcScript statement); for example, the argument contains incorrect characters. -1304 <UPROCERR_UNKNOWN_CONTEXT> Function not allowed, unknown context. For example, $formmodality was used without the InstName argument and no instance is current. Use Use in Form components. Example: Using $formmodality The following example puts a message in the message frame that indicates if the current instance was started as a modal or non-modal form: if ($formmodality = 1) putmess "%%$instancename is modal." else putmess "%%$instancename is non-modal" endif Copy Related Links activate Calls an operation on a component instance. newinstance Creates a new instance of a component.
